description: Phase 1b - PDF Support: Add PDF file ingestion to the Vault app using lopdf for Rust-side text extraction. PDFs flow through the existing detection and masking pipeline. Includes page markers, extraction warnings, .txt export, and experimental masked PDF export.

userStories.title: Add warning and raw_pdf_bytes columns to items table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need schema changes to support PDF-specific data so that extraction warnings and original PDF bytes can be stored.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'warning' TEXT column (nullable) to items table using ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or equivalent idempotent migration
Add 'raw_pdf_bytes' BLOB column (nullable) to items table using ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS or equivalent idempotent migration
Existing items are unaffected (new columns default to NULL)
Update the get_item command response struct to include 'warning' (Option<String>) field
Update the save_item command to accept an optional 'warning' (Option<String>) parameter
Update the save_item command to accept an optional 'raw_pdf_bytes' (Option<Vec<u8>>) parameter
Update the list_items response struct to include 'warning' (Option<String>) so the file list can show warning indicators
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: Use ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN wrapped in a check to make it idempotent. rusqlite will error if the column already exists, so catch that error and ignore it.

userStories.title: Add lopdf dependency and extract_pdf_text Tauri command
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Rust command that extracts text from PDF bytes so the frontend can process PDF content through the existing masking pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Add lopdf crate to Cargo.toml
Create extract_pdf_text Tauri command that accepts PDF content as Vec<u8>
Command loads the PDF via lopdf::Document::load_from() using a Cursor over the bytes
Text is extracted page by page using document.extract_text(&[page_number]) for each page in document.get_pages()

Pages are concatenated with '

--- Page N ---

' markers between them
Command returns a struct with: text (String), page_count (u32), has_warning (bool)
If the entire PDF yields zero text (empty string after trimming), return an error: 'No extractable text found. This may be a scanned document.'
If some pages have text and others don't, return partial text with has_warning set to true
If lopdf fails to parse the file entirely, return an error: 'Failed to parse PDF file.'
Command is registered in the Tauri builder via generate_handler!
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: lopdf::Document::load_from() accepts impl Read. Use std::io::Cursor<Vec<u8>> to wrap the bytes. document.get_pages() returns a BTreeMap<u32, ObjectId> — iterate sorted keys for page order.

userStories.title: Add export_masked_pdf Tauri command
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Rust command that creates a masked version of a PDF by replacing entity values with tokens in the original document.
acceptanceCriteria: Create export_masked_pdf Tauri command that accepts: original PDF bytes (Vec<u8>), a list of replacement pairs ({original: String, token: String}), and uses a native save dialog
Command loads the original PDF via lopdf::Document::load_from()
For each replacement pair, calls document.replace_text() to substitute original values with token strings
Opens a native save dialog with default filename '[name]_masked.pdf' via tauri_plugin_dialog
Saves the modified PDF to the chosen path via document.save_to()
Returns Ok(true) on success, Ok(false) if user cancels the dialog
If replace_text() or save fails, return an error string describing the failure
Define a ReplacementPair serde struct with 'original' and 'token' String fields
Command is registered in the Tauri builder via generate_handler!
cargo check passes
userStories.notes: lopdf replace_text() may not work for all font encodings. The frontend should catch errors and fall back to .txt export.

userStories.title: Update drop zone to accept PDF files and wire ingestion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drop PDF files into the vault so they are automatically extracted, masked, and stored.
acceptanceCriteria: Drop zone accepts .pdf files in addition to .txt, .md, .csv, .json
Drop zone instructional text updated to include .pdf in the list of supported formats
When a .pdf file is dropped, read file as ArrayBuffer via FileReader (not as text)
Convert ArrayBuffer to Uint8Array and pass to invoke('extract_pdf_text', { content: Array.from(uint8array) })

On success: feed extracted text through detect() and mask() pipeline, then call save_item with file_type 'pdf', the extracted text as raw_content, masked text as masked_content, warning if has_warning is true, and the original PDF bytes as raw_pdf_bytes
On extraction error: show error toast with the error message, do not save to vault
After saving, refresh the file list
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
userStories.notes: PDF files must be read as ArrayBuffer (not text). The bytes are sent to Rust as Vec<u8> via invoke. Non-PDF files continue to use FileReader.readAsText() as before.

userStories.title: Display extraction warnings for PDF items
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a warning when a PDF was only partially extracted so I know the results may be incomplete.
acceptanceCriteria: When a PDF item with a non-null warning is selected, display a yellow warning banner above the content viewer text: 'Some pages in this PDF could not be extracted. Results may be incomplete.'
The warning text comes from the item's warning field in the database
Warning banner is styled distinctly (yellow/amber background, warning icon or prefix)
Warning banner is visible in both Masked and Original view modes
Non-PDF items or PDFs without warnings do not show any banner
Items in the file list sidebar show a small warning indicator (e.g., ⚠ icon) next to the file name if they have a warning
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools

userStories.title: Style page markers in content viewer for PDF items
userStories.description: As a user, I want page boundaries clearly visible in the content viewer so I can understand the document structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Page markers ('--- Page N ---') in both Masked and Original views are visually styled differently from content text
Page markers are rendered as centered text with muted color and horizontal rules above and below
Page markers are NOT treated as entity-detectable content: the buildHighlightedSegments() function skips page marker regions so they are not highlighted or detected as entities
Entity highlights work correctly on the text between page markers
PDF items show a .pdf badge in the file list (visually distinct from .txt, .md, etc.)
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
userStories.notes: Page marker detection can use a simple regex: /\n?--- Page \d+ ---\n?/g to identify marker positions in the text.

userStories.title: Export PDF masked content as .txt file
userStories.description: As a user, I want to export the masked version of a PDF as a text file so I can use it externally.
acceptanceCriteria: When exporting a PDF item via the existing Export button, the save dialog defaults to [original_name]_masked.txt (not .pdf)
The exported .txt file contains the masked text including page markers
Clipboard copy works the same as other file types (copies full masked text)
Batch zip export includes PDF items as [name]_masked.txt files
The existing export_file Tauri command is reused — the frontend passes 'txt' as the file extension for PDF items
pnpm check passes
userStories.notes: This mostly works already — just need to ensure that when file_type is 'pdf', the export uses .txt extension instead of .pdf.

userStories.title: Add experimental masked PDF export button
userStories.description: As a user, I want the option to export a masked PDF that preserves the original layout so I can share redacted documents in their original format.
acceptanceCriteria: When viewing a PDF item, an additional button appears in the toolbar: 'Export PDF (experimental)'
The button is only visible for items where file_type is 'pdf'
Clicking the button calls invoke('export_masked_pdf') with the original PDF bytes from raw_pdf_bytes and the entity mappings (original_value -> token) from the item's entities
On success, show a toast: 'Masked PDF exported'
On error, show a toast: 'PDF export failed — falling back to text export' and trigger the standard .txt export
The button is labeled with '(experimental)' to set expectations
pnpm check passes
Verify in browser using dev tools
userStories.notes: raw_pdf_bytes comes from the get_item response. It may need to be base64-decoded on the frontend before passing back to Rust as an array.
